lines.text: Review that a huge increase was expected in after the May 1952 Mars approach,
and this was the actual case. This issue was printed December 1951.
Reports continued to come in at an even rate until Nov. 1951, when an un-
precedented new object appeared, the Green Fireballs. Our study of all known
American aircraft and missiles, and meteor history showed these objects were no
known flying weapon or meteor known in astronomical history.
We believe the fireballs are allied somehow to the saucers, having a common
unknown origin; that their great brilliancy (differing from saucers unobtrusive-
ness meant they were probably some sort of signals, the green color as a similar-
ity to Earth color seen from space (due to large amount of surface covered by
water.) The meaning of these signals is not clear, although Donald Keyhoes new
book states they may be warning us in handling atomic weapons which were being
tested at time of fireball sightings!
Another object we have found, is a rare brilliant color-changing light seen
about 15 times in the last 7 years. It is usually described as hovering from 10
minutes to 2 hours, continually changing from red to green to white. Its
great brilliancy is also very different from the ordinary stealthy saucers, and
we believe it is also a signal device of some sort.
FUTURE SAUCER ACTIVITY
On the basis of our present conclusions, we expect a great increase in the
saucer reports to begin again with the approach of Mars in summer of 1954. Re-
ports should increase during late spring, and reach their peak in late summer,
with more reports of the occasional "landings" reported in the past 3 years,
and total numbers of dozens a day similar to July 1952.
We therefore appreciate any publicity aid given us in requesting public
response in reports, that will help us maintain the best coverage in the coming
important months of 1954.
(This summary of the History, work, and conclusions of the Flying
Saucer Researchers was written by Elliott Rockmore, editor of
group magazine and head of the organization. Permission is given
to quote any part of this 5 page release, but we would appreciate
being credited as the source of material. Requests of the readers
of this release to send in eyewitness and newspaper reports would
also greatly aid our work.)
